---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Do not edit it directly. -->

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%",
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 4.5,
  dpi = 150
)
```

# forrest <img src="man/figures/logo.png" align="right" height="139" alt="" />

<!-- badges: start -->
[![Lifecycle: experimental](https://img.shields.io/badge/lifecycle-experimental-orange.svg)](https://lifecycle.r-lib.org/articles/stages.html#experimental)
[![CRAN status](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/forrest)](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=forrest)
[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/lorenzoFabbri/forrest/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/lorenzoFabbri/forrest/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![Codecov test coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/lorenzoFabbri/forrest/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://app.codecov.io/gh/lorenzoFabbri/forrest?branch=main)
[![](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/forrest)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=forrest)
[![Buy Me A Coffee](https://img.shields.io/badge/Buy%20Me%20A%20Coffee-%E2%98%95-yellow.svg)](https://www.buymeacoffee.com/epilorenzo)
<!-- badges: end -->

`forrest` creates publication-ready forest plots with a single dependency —
[tinyplot](https://github.com/grantmcdermott/tinyplot). It is designed for
epidemiologists and data scientists who need clean, customisable forest plots
without pulling in a large dependency tree.

**Key features:**

- Minimal dependency footprint (only `tinyplot`)
- Subgroup headers and spacer rows via `NA` estimate rows
- Summary estimates rendered as filled diamonds
- Study weights scale point size (meta-analysis convention)
- Confidence intervals clipped at axis limits with directional arrows
- Group coloring with automatic Okabe-Ito legend
- Optional text columns (OR, p-value, …) alongside the plot
- Log-scale x-axis for ratio measures
- Works with `data.frame`, `tibble`, and `data.table`

## Installation

```{r, eval = FALSE}
# Development version from GitHub:
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("lorenzoFabbri/forrest")
```

## Quick start

```{r basic}
library(forrest)

dat <- data.frame(
  study = c(
    "Smith 2018", "Jones 2019", "Brown 2020",
    "Garcia 2021", "Lee 2022", "Pooled"
  ),
  estimate = c(-0.18, 0.05, -0.12, -0.08, 0.02, -0.07),
  lower = c(-0.38, -0.12, -0.30, -0.24, -0.14, -0.16),
  upper = c(0.02, 0.22, 0.06, 0.08, 0.18, 0.02),
  is_sum = c(FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE),
  weight = c(12.1, 4.5, 9.8, 7.2, 5.3, NA)
)

forrest(
  dat,
  estimate   = "estimate",
  lower      = "lower",
  upper      = "upper",
  label      = "study",
  is_summary = "is_sum",
  weight     = "weight",
  xlab       = "Mean difference (95% CI)"
)
```

## Subgroup analysis with text columns

```{r subgroups, fig.width = 9, fig.height = 5.5}
sub <- data.frame(
  label = c(
    "Sex", "  Female", "  Male", "  Pooled",
    "", "Region", "  North", "  South", "  Pooled"
  ),
  est = c(NA, -0.10, -0.05, -0.07, NA, NA, -0.12, -0.02, -0.07),
  lo = c(NA, -0.22, -0.18, -0.16, NA, NA, -0.28, -0.12, -0.16),
  hi = c(NA, 0.02, 0.08, 0.02, NA, NA, 0.04, 0.08, 0.02),
  is_sum = c(FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE),
  or_text = c(
    "", "-0.10 (-0.22, 0.02)", "-0.05 (-0.18, 0.08)",
    "-0.07 (-0.16, 0.02)", "",
    "", "-0.12 (-0.28, 0.04)", "-0.02 (-0.12, 0.08)",
    "-0.07 (-0.16, 0.02)"
  )
)

forrest(
  sub,
  estimate   = "est",
  lower      = "lo",
  upper      = "hi",
  label      = "label",
  is_summary = "is_sum",
  header     = "Subgroup",
  cols       = c("Estimate (95% CI)" = "or_text"),
  widths     = c(2.2, 4, 2.5)
)
```

## Log scale for ratio measures

```{r logscale}
or_dat <- data.frame(
  study  = c("Adams 2016", "Baker 2018", "Chen 2019", "Pooled"),
  est    = c(1.45, 0.88, 1.21, 1.09),
  lo     = c(1.10, 0.65, 0.92, 0.94),
  hi     = c(1.91, 1.19, 1.59, 1.26),
  is_sum = c(FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE)
)

forrest(
  or_dat,
  estimate   = "est",
  lower      = "lo",
  upper      = "hi",
  label      = "study",
  is_summary = "is_sum",
  log_scale  = TRUE,
  ref_line   = 1,
  xlab       = "Odds ratio (95% CI)"
)
```

## Compared with other packages

| Feature | forrest | forestplot | forestploter | ggforestplot |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Minimal dependencies | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Study weight sizing | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Subgroup headers | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Summary diamonds | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Text columns | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| CI clipping with arrows | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Group coloring + legend | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Log-scale axis | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| data.table support | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Actively maintained | ✅ | ⚠️ | ✅ | ❌ |
